refPatterns.name: Canonical knowledge-file taxonomy
refPatterns.description: Five named free-form surfaces (architecture / commands / patterns / gotchas / decisions), each with a one-line editorial charter and surgical-edit discipline. Suggests Setlist's project_digests schema could grow a small named catalog of digest_kind values without migration.

refPatterns.name: PostToolUse JSONL event-feed ingest
refPatterns.description: Low-cost tool-call event stream (Edit/Write/Bash/NotebookEdit; skip Read/Glob/Grep as noisy) appended as JSONL with rotation discipline (cap at 500, keep last 300). Gives Setlist a uniform event-ingest surface so interactive sessions stop producing zero reinforcement signal.

refPatterns.name: Stateless read + cursor-based incremental reflection
refPatterns.description: Per-invocation lightweight update layer: read what's known, tail the delta since last cursor, reinforce or contradict, advance the cursor. Separates continuous incremental reinforcement from threshold-triggered deep consolidation. Gated on the event-feed pattern landing first.

refPatterns.name: rescan_project as first-class reset
refPatterns.description: One named admin operation that archives (soft-delete, not destruction) all project-scoped memories + refreshes the digest + clears reinforcement history. Replaces N separate forget + digest-refresh calls for post-refactor cleanup while preserving audit trail.

refPatterns.name: Bounded-file discipline as principle
refPatterns.description: Cross-cutting rule: every free-form surface carries an explicit token/line ceiling, enforced at write time, selected for compactness not comprehensiveness. Generalizes the existing digest ceiling to cover the event feed, any future digest-kind catalog, and any future free-form surface.
